scope: SYBEORG only; no Radi / House of Meroe files, targets, drafts, accounts, or campaigns inspected or used.
researchMethod: OSM/Nominatim public listing discovery across Iowa and nearby Midwest markets, direct homepage fetches for candidates with website fields, and duplicate checks across mockups.json, master-send-board.json, and prior daily batches. No outbound contact was made.

publicListingSignals: Title: Lincoln HVAC, Electrical, & Plumbing Services | Star City Heating & Cooling
12 tel links and one form found in fetched HTML
selectedLeads.publicEmailSource: none_found; do not use example@domain.com as contact
selectedLeads.diagnosis: A polished HVAC/electrical/plumbing site may still leak trust if placeholder contact text is present in shipped page/source. This is best handled as a helpful technical note, not a redesign critique.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Helpful technical cleanup note first; if they engage, offer a seasonal HVAC/electrical service-page check using existing brand and real proof.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Specific, verifiable bug-style outreach is easier to accept than a generic web pitch.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $300 technical/contact-path cleanup or $699 focused seasonal service-page concept after confirmation.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Service-specific landing pages, tracking-ready call/form routes, seasonal offer pages, paid-ad readiness cleanup.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; precise placeholder/contact-path alert is the creative angle.

realAssetsFound.title: Lincoln HVAC, Electrical, & Plumbing Services | Star City Heating & Cooling

unverifiedClaimsAvoided: real users definitely see the placeholder
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup QA because no mockup was created. Manual browser/source review recommended before outreach.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi Star City team — this is Majid with SYBEORG. Quick helpful note, not a pitch blast. While checking Lincoln HVAC/electrical pages, I saw your site is live with phone/form paths, but the page source I fetched included a placeholder-looking email string: example@domain.com. It may not be visible to customers, but it is worth a quick cleanup check because it can undermine trust or confuse technical/contact audits. Want me to send the exact note? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: Title: Residential & Commercial Plumbing & Heating Services | Tony's Plumbing
One form found; water-heater text found; no tel links found in initial HTML fetch
selectedLeads.diagnosis: The business has a credible site; the nearer monetizable leak is a focused high-intent plumbing/heating page that keeps mobile visitors on one call/request path.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Dedicated water-heater or urgent plumbing request page matched to the current brand with owner-approved service details/photos.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Plumbing jobs justify a narrow conversion page and the pitch is additive rather than critical.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $699 service-specific landing page concept; $300 contact-path review if they want a lighter first step.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Service pages, call tracking, seasonal campaign pages, Google Business Profile website-link/service cleanup.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; a generic plumbing page would not meet the quality standard against an active credible site.

realAssetsFound.title: Residential & Commercial Plumbing & Heating Services | Tony's Plumbing

selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup QA because no mockup was created. Manual brand/asset review required before any visual concept.
selectedLeads.draftSubject: Cedar Falls plumbing landing page idea
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi Tony's Plumbing & Heating team — Majid with SYBEORG here. I came across your Cedar Falls site today; this is not a ‘you need a new website’ note. The specific opportunity I noticed is a focused water-heater or urgent plumbing page that keeps mobile visitors on one next step — call or request service — instead of asking them to browse the broader site. If useful, I can make a no-obligation concept that matches your current brand and uses only details you approve. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: Title: Expert HVAC Service | Lincoln, NE
Appointment, financing, drain, and water-heater text found; two tel links; no form in first HTML fetch
selectedLeads.diagnosis: A seasonal HVAC visitor may need a single-action request path for one service/offer rather than broader site browsing.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Summer cooling or high-intent request landing page with clear call/appointment routing and tracking-ready structure.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: HVAC economics, financing, and appointment messaging make a narrow conversion page a natural add-on.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $699 seasonal HVAC landing page concept matched to the existing brand.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Call tracking, seasonal ad pages, maintenance-plan pages, Google Business Profile service/category cleanup.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; active-site candidate needs brand-specific review.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup QA because no mockup was created. Manual visual/contact route review required.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi Bryant Air Conditioning team — I was reviewing Lincoln HVAC pages and saw your site is already live with appointment/financing paths, so I would not pitch this as a rebuild. The narrower idea is a seasonal service page — for example a cooling or high-intent request path — that keeps visitors on one action and is easier to measure if you ever run ads or local campaigns. Worth me putting together a no-obligation concept using only approved Bryant details? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: Title: Epp Foundation Repair & Basement Waterproofing | Lincoln, Omaha
Estimate/contact/financing text found; five tel links; one form
selectedLeads.diagnosis: The site is substantial, so the potential leak is matching expensive foundation/waterproofing clicks to a narrow diagnostic/estimate path instead of broad homepage navigation.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Basement water or foundation crack diagnostic landing page routing visitors to the right estimate path with tracked intent categories.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: High-ticket foundation leads can justify a specific conversion asset; outreach can be additive and respectful.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $699 diagnostic landing-page concept or $300 conversion-path audit first.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Multiple diagnostic/service pages, campaign tracking, financing/estimate flow testing, retargeting/paid-ad readiness.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; large/polished site requires deeper manual review before visual work.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup QA because no mockup was created. Manual visual/conversion-path review required.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi Epp team — Majid with SYBEORG. I came across your foundation/waterproofing site while reviewing Midwest home-service pages. Your site already has clear contact/estimate paths, so this is not a rebuild note. The opportunity I noticed is a narrower diagnostic landing page for one high-intent path — basement water, wall cracks, or settlement — that can route visitors into the right estimate request and make campaign traffic easier to measure. Worth polishing this up as a no-obligation concept? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: Public listing pass surfaced name/category but no website field
No phone/email/hours surfaced in this pass
selectedLeads.diagnosis: If the public listing truly lacks a website/contact path, roofing prospects may not get enough trust or next-step context during storm/repair demand windows.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: GMB/profile cleanup audit first; roof-storm landing page only after official contact route and facts are verified.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Roofing urgency is monetizable, but this lead should not be contacted until contact/ownership facts are verified.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $300 profile/contact-path cleanup after verification; optional landing-page concept later.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Storm-response landing page, photo/project proof page, call tracking, paid-ad readiness.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; without verified phone/site/proof, a roofing mockup would be generic.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup QA because no mockup was created. Manual GMB/contact verification required.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi Anchor Roofing team — I was reviewing Omaha roofing listings and noticed the public listing source I checked did not show a clear owned website/contact path. If that is accurate, a small storm-response page or profile cleanup could make the next step easier for homeowners. Worth sending a no-obligation idea? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

mockupsSkippedReason: Quality standard: selected leads were polished existing-site opportunities, helpful-alert candidates, or insufficiently verified profile leads. A generic mockup would not meet the SYBEORG quality bar.
